summary: A member whose frontmatter fails to load (no name, malformed YAML) must become a collected error Diagnostic naming the file, never a thrown Err that aborts the whole check run.

edit.description: read_file_unit's YamlFrontmatter/None-format arm (502-530) calls frontmatter::Member::from_source_rooted with `?`, and resolve_kind_units's two file-discovery loops (606-654) call read_file_unit with `?` in turn — a FrontmatterError::{NoId,NoNamedFieldId,Malformed} on any one file aborts the whole kind's resolution via miette::Result. Catch those three variants at this boundary and collect an error-severity Diagnostic (naming the file and the fault) instead, continuing the loop over the remaining files; thread the diagnostics out (e.g. resolve_kind_units/kind_units_and_features return them alongside the units). Leave read_file_unit's other error paths (layout_unit, json_manifest, toml_document, and frontmatter's own Io/NotUtf8) on their existing hard-fail `?` — outside the verified gap (the probe's crashes were specifically frontmatter::{no_named_field_id,malformed}).

kind_units_and_features is called from gate's builtin-kind loop (~140-159) and custom-kind loop (~224-233); once compose.rs's chain returns collected load-fault diagnostics alongside a kind's units, merge them into the run's `diagnostics` vec at both call sites.

a_malformed_frontmatter_block_fails_loud_naming_the_file (136-175) currently asserts the run aborts with zero github-format findings emitted (comment: 'aborts loud; it must not emit field findings') — that is the behavior this entry reverses. Rewrite: the run still fails (!run.ok), output still names the file and 'mapping', but `run.findings()` now carries a real finding naming the file rather than being empty. Add a second case: a harness with the malformed member plus one clean, unrelated member — the clean member must still be discovered and checked in the same run (the actual 4/9-harness crash the probe caught).

tests.asserts: a malformed or nameless member's load fault is a collected error Diagnostic naming the file, and every other member in the harness is still discovered and checked in the same run

acceptance: check over a harness with one malformed/nameless member plus N clean ones exits non-zero, reports a finding naming the bad file, and still validates the N clean members instead of aborting the whole run.
notes: Verified on disk: compose.rs 502-530/606-654 propagate FrontmatterError via `?`, matching the probe's 4/9-harness crash. gate_fail_loud.rs:136 currently encodes today's abort-with-no-findings as deliberate Invariant-6 compliance; Invariant 6 is 'loud', not 'abort-first-file' — an error Diagnostic is still loud and matches rust.md's own Diagnostic-not-Err rule. scoped at 9d4ce81.

summary: The session-start reporter drops every non-error diagnostic from additionalContext, so e.g. drift (config.stale, Warn-severity) can silently never reach the session.

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: context() (105-139) filters diagnostics to Severity::Error only under 'Blocking findings', and returns None (fully quiet payload) whenever no Error diagnostic and no Announcement are present — so a run whose only diagnostics are Warn (drift's config_stale, install::gate_installed, coverage_note) is indistinguishable from a genuinely clean run. Extend context() to also surface non-error diagnostics (e.g. a separate 'Advisory findings' section) whenever any diagnostic exists, so the payload is never silent while a real finding exists; the exit-code verdict (session-start never fails the run) stays unchanged — only visibility changes.

a_clean_harness_is_quiet (326-341, inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests) asserts an advisory-only diagnostic set stays quiet ('Advisory-only is still a clean *contract* — quiet') — that assertion is exactly the bug distribution.md's 'never silently passes' forbids; rewrite it so the advisory now surfaces in additionalContext, distinct from the still-absent notify-and-approve instruction a failing (error-severity) contract carries.

tests.asserts: a run whose only diagnostic is Warn-severity (e.g. drift's config.stale) still carries it in additionalContext, distinguishable from a genuinely clean run with zero diagnostics

acceptance: temper check . --reporter session-start's additionalContext names every diagnostic the terminal reporter would show, drift included, whatever its severity; a genuinely clean harness (zero diagnostics, no announcement) is still quiet.
notes: Verified: reporter.rs context() 105-112 filters strictly on Severity::Error; gate.rs 465-469's own comment confirms config_stale/gate_installed are deliberately Warn 'because the session-start reporter ignores warn severity' — the exact mechanism the inbox repro caught (drift invisible, other announced context visible). Fix changes visibility only, never the advisory exit posture. scoped at 9d4ce81.

summary: command's default contract still imports skill's whole strict name/description profile (all required, guidance text says 'skill'), though builtins.md now rules command requires no field.

edit.description: commandDefaultContract (1401-1403) is defined as skillDefaultContract.filter(exclude name-matches-dir) — every one of the remaining eleven clauses (name/description required, min_len, max_len, allowed_chars, shape, deny, plus compatibility's max_len) is exactly the 'strict name/description profile' the 07-22 spec ruling now keeps to skill alone. Replace with an empty array (`[]`) and rewrite the doc comment (1393-1400) off the updated builtins.md bullet and code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands (retrieved 2026-07-22) rather than the superseded 2026-07-15 skills-docs cite.

Generated data (see file header) — regenerate by re-running the memberless emit so the compiled command.* clause rows (204-338 today: required/min_len/max_len/allowed_chars/shape/deny over name and description, plus compatibility/extent/forbidden_keys/glob-valid) drop to whichever survive an empty commandDefaultContract. Never hand-edit.

claude_code_command()'s doc comment (222-226) claims 'the skill's field schema by import' as the command kind's contract source — no longer true once commandDefaultContract is empty; correct the doc comment.

Module header (1-7) frames command as carrying 'the skill's field schema (imported, not re-derived)'; a_command_member_extracts_the_skills_declared_field_schema (95) asserts that import directly. Rewrite both for the empty contract: a command file with no frontmatter fields at all (not even name/description) must check clean.

Data-driven over commandDefaultContract (37-38, 67) — its SDK-build-and-byte-compare is what forces the builtin_lock.toml regenerate above; no manual edit expected here, listed for the ripple it exercises.

tests.asserts: a .claude/commands/*.md file carrying no frontmatter fields at all passes check clean, per the updated builtins.md command bullet

acceptance: check over a command file with no name/description/any field is clean; no command-kind guidance text says 'skill'; tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs's byte-compare passes against the regenerated embedded lock.
notes: Verified on disk: sdk/src/builtins.ts:1401 commandDefaultContract = skillDefaultContract minus only name-matches-dir; builtin_lock.toml:204-338 carries the compiled command.* rows. Closes probe findings 6 (14 false-positive hard-fails) and 7 (the 'skill' guidance mislabel) together — same clauses, same fix. scoped at 9d4ce81.

summary: emit_program strict-parses the SDK payload before checking its seam version, so a shape-incompatible payload surfaces as an opaque payload_parse error, never a version-mismatch diagnostic.

edit.description: emit_program (944-958) runs serde_json::from_str::<Payload>(&json) directly, raising DriftError::PayloadParse (954) on any shape mismatch; the SEAM_VERSION comparison (1034, inside emit()) is only reached once that strict parse already succeeded. Peek the payload's `version` field off the raw JSON first (a minimal pre-parse, e.g. into a `{version: u32}`-shaped value or serde_json::Value) and return DriftError::UnsupportedSeamVersion immediately when it doesn't match SEAM_VERSION, before attempting the full strict deserialize. Extract the peek-then-parse into its own function so it is unit-testable without spawning `node`.

tests.asserts: a raw JSON payload string whose `version` field mismatches SEAM_VERSION, and whose remaining shape is also incompatible with Payload, yields UnsupportedSeamVersion naming both versions, never PayloadParse

acceptance: A stale-binary-vs-newer-SDK mismatch always reports a version diagnostic naming both versions, never the generic 'invalid type' serde error the inbox's debugging detour hit.
notes: Verified: drift.rs 952-958 (emit_program) strict-parses before any version check; SEAM_VERSION compare lives inside emit() at 1034, unreached on a parse failure. tests/emit.rs:406 an_unsupported_seam_version_is_a_clear_refusal only covers the already-parsed-struct case (drift::emit called directly), not the raw-JSON path this entry fixes. scoped at 9d4ce81.

summary: install.gate-installed fires as pure noise on any foreign harness with no .temper/, and even when it legitimately fires it counts un-noted files without naming them.

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: gate_installed (520-578): (1) it always calls evaluate_placements or place_settings_only regardless of whether `.temper/` exists on disk at all; place_settings_only (588) unconditionally checks whether .claude/settings.json has the hook wired, firing on any repo lacking it even with zero temper adoption. Add an early `if !temper_dir.is_dir() { return Vec::new(); }` before line 523. (2) the notes/modelines tally (534-568) collapses to a bare count in the rendered message (570-577); thread each un-noted InstallEntry's own `path` into the message instead of only counting outcomes.

gate_installed_never_scaffolds_and_reflects_represented_vs_not (949) and its neighbors (945-976): add a case for a harness with no `.temper/` directory at all, asserting gate_installed is empty; update an existing drifted-notes case to assert the message names the specific un-noted file(s), not just a count.

tests.asserts: gate_installed is silent on a harness carrying no .temper/ directory, and names the specific un-noted file(s) when it fires on an adopted-but-drifted harness

acceptance: Checking a plain foreign repo with no .temper/ never emits install.gate-installed; an adopted harness missing managed-by notes reports which files, not just a count.
notes: Verified: install.rs 520-529 gate_installed calls evaluate_placements/place_settings_only unconditionally; place_settings_only (588) checks settings.json's hook wiring with no adoption check at all. Count-only message at 570-577 (parts.join). Closes probe finding 8 and dogfood finding 1 together — same function. scoped at 9d4ce81.

summary: The placed SessionStart/guard hooks invoke bare `temper ...`; with no binary on PATH they die command-not-found silently, so the gate can never report its own absence.

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: SESSION_START_COMMAND (92) and GUARD_COMMAND (138) are bare `temper ...` strings Claude Code's hook runs through a shell. Add a PATH-resolvability guard ahead of the real invocation (e.g. a `command -v temper` prefix that fails loud, naming `temper` as the missing binary, before attempting to run it) so a fresh clone with no `temper` on PATH gets a visible, named failure rather than a silent shell command-not-found — per distribution.md's fail-loud invariant ('a placement that cannot run the engine errors, never silently skips').
~8 call sites assert the literal command strings verbatim (lines 78, 244, 281, 322, 440, 768, 920, 924) — update each to the new guarded form.

Line 360 splits SESSION_START_COMMAND on whitespace into an argv array for a direct-exec repro; a compound shell-guarded command breaks that split. Re-drive it through an actual shell invocation (or the hook's real dispatch path) instead.
Line 64 asserts the plugin-shipped hooks.json carries the literal SESSION_START_COMMAND string verbatim; update to the new guarded form.

tests.asserts: the placed SessionStart/guard hook command, run with `temper` absent from PATH, exits non-zero and prints a message naming `temper` as the missing binary rather than a bare shell command-not-found

acceptance: A fresh clone with the hook wired but no `temper` on PATH gets a named, visible failure instead of a silent no-op.
notes: Verified: install.rs 92/138 SESSION_START_COMMAND/GUARD_COMMAND are bare `temper ...`; ~8 tests across install.rs/session_start.rs/bundle.rs assert the literal strings — the honest ripple. Serialized behind GATE-INSTALLED-NAMES-FILES-SUPPRESS-UNADOPTED: both entries edit install.rs, and the pending-entry rule bars two open entries sharing a file. scoped at 9d4ce81.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at ac40c72, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff a23269d..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.

summary: 0045 also frees guidance onto a field declaration, no predicate — deferred: none of the six promoted built-in notes targets a single field, so there is no consumer yet

gate.reason: 0045's Consequences name field-declaration guidance beside kind-level, but the SDK's only per-field metadata today is a clause's `field:` target (predicate-bound) or `EdgeField` (edge-typed only) — no 'field declaration' list a bare advisory could attach to. None of the six promoted notes (GUIDANCE-BUILTIN-NOTES-PROMOTE) target a single field, inventing the mechanism now is speculative scope with no kind to exercise it ('An export earns its consumer', engineering.md). Revive when a field wants counsel no clause carries.

edit.description: Anticipated: a field-name-keyed guidance fact on `KindFacts`, analogous to `EdgeField`'s field-name-keyed list (196) but for arbitrary fields — shape unset; no field in any built-in kind wants one yet.
Anticipated: a `KindFactRow` column (or sibling row) carrying per-field guidance/cite, following the `to_table`/`from_table` pattern GUIDANCE-KIND-SDK-LOCK-FACT establishes for the kind-level pair.

Anticipated: `emit()`'s per-property `description` (156-172) folds field-declaration guidance in alongside a clause's, the same 'join into one description' discipline `push_description` (316-329) already runs for several guided clauses on one field.
Anticipated: field-level narration extends `narrate_governing_contract` (374-402) or a sibling to print a field's declared guidance.

acceptance: A field declaring `guidance` (no predicate) surfaces it via `schema` hover and `explain`, never gating — provable once a real kind needs one.
notes: 0045 Consequences name field-declaration guidance beside kind-level; this waits for a driver, the same 'no consumer' bar prior deferred entries in this queue's history have held to. scoped at 946e303.
